The BMW 4' G22 Coupe is the G22 generation of the 4 line for cars. In Europe, this generation was produced from 2020-2021. This page supports OEM catalog use for the European-spec configuration, where precise variant identification matters when matching diagrams to a specific vehicle build.

For the Europe market, the lineup covers both diesel and petrol powertrains across multiple output levels, with four- and six-cylinder options depending on the variant. Engine identifiers used in this region may appear in the catalog as codes such as B47D or B58D, and the same nameplate can map to different technical details by year. Drivetrain availability is split between rear-wheel drive and xDrive all-wheel drive, so confirming the driven axle configuration is a key step before selecting related diagram views.

The classifier tree above is designed to guide you through the catalog using a system-based structure rather than a flat parts list. As you move through the tree, keep your vehicle’s production year and variant context in mind so the diagrams remain aligned with the correct European specification. Where multiple technical routes exist for the same model name, relying on the precise variant identifiers shown alongside the diagrams helps prevent mismatches.

Within the diagrams, information is grouped around major mechanical assemblies, service-related groupings, electrical components, chassis-level items, and body and interior areas, with cross-references that reflect how the vehicle is configured. Because this is the Coupe body, diagram scope and reference points are aligned to that body style, and not to other shapes that may exist in the wider 4 family. Transmission application is also variant-dependent in the catalog, so staying consistent with the exact engine/drive designation helps keep the diagram context coherent across adjacent groupings.

One area that often requires additional attention on this generation is the fuel system. Its configuration can vary across production years and regional specifications, so staying within the correct system context helps avoid mixing incompatible assemblies when navigating related diagrams.

If you have the VIN, it can help narrow down the correct build configuration and may refine selection when more than one European variant appears similar on the surface. For BMW, you can use the BMW VIN decoder and then compare the decoded details to the identifiers shown in the catalog.

Use the diagrams as the primary reference, and verify each selection against the vehicle’s year and configuration before finalizing compatibility. This approach is especially helpful on the BMW 4' G22 Coupe in Europe, where small specification differences can change how related diagram groupings are presented.
